event: did not use any IV drugs in the past 12 months
extraction of all maxillary teeth 5 months prior
mandibular dental infection 3 months before presentation
oxygen saturation of 99% on room air
denied weakness of the lower extremities
prominent holosystolic murmur at the apex
petechial rash scattered on the abdomen, palms, and soles of the feet
neurological exam was remarkable for an encephalopathy
admitted to the intensive care unit
started on empiric antibiotic treatment with vancomycin and meropenem
presented left hemi-neglect and right gaze deviation
liver and kidney functions were within normal
imaging findings showed innumerable acute to subacute embolic infarcts in both cerebral and cerebellar hemispheres
proximal right internal carotid artery T-shaped acute thrombus
occlusion of the right middle cerebral artery
subarachnoid hemorrhage in the right parietal region and around the posterior aspect of the midbrain
transthoracic echocardiogram (TTE) showed echo densities on the tricuspid, aortic, and mitral valves
blood culture grew S. marcescens in two bottles
S. marcescens isolate was pansensitive susceptible to ceftriaxone, cefepime, ceftazidime, meropenem, gentamicin, tobramycin, amikacin, ciprofloxacin, and levofloxacin
patient was a poor candidate for surgery
neurological status of the patient continued to deteriorate
presented no cough or gag reflex
patient was discharged to hospice care
